data.description:
Rec 2 (+ Rec 1 Tier A) of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risk R5). CLI reads go bash ticket-show.sh/ticket-list.sh -> python3 heredoc -> ticket_reducer while library/MCP reads go _reads.py -> ticket_reducer in-process: arg parsing, id-resolution dispatch, and error-message STRINGS are written twice (_reads.py hand-replicates subprocess stderr text so parity tests pass). Worse, freshness diverges: bash-path commands run _ensure_initialized (fetch/merge <=1/min) but in-process reads do not — so the MCP interface (the primary agent surface) has the STALEST reads despite real-time propagation being a first-order requirement.

Work:
- Point the dispatcher read arms (show/list/ready/search/deps) at the existing Python implementations in _reads.py; delete the bash read shims + their heredocs.
- Make freshness an explicit interface-uniform policy: add the <=1/min best-effort fetch (with an env/--no-sync opt-out) to the native read path so all three interfaces share one freshness contract; document it.
- Delete tests/interfaces/test_native_read_parity.py once the second implementation is gone (a parity test outliving its second impl is dead weight).

BREAKING-WINDOW: output text/freshness semantics may shift; land while there are no real consumers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One read implementation serves CLI, library, and MCP (bash read shims deleted)
- [ ] Read-freshness policy is uniform across interfaces, opt-out documented
- [ ] Schema conformance + interface parity suites green; native-read parity test retired
- [ ] No error-message strings hand-mirrored between Python and bash
data.title: Collapse the dual read path: CLI reads via _reads.py + uniform read-freshness policy
